title: Process a Full Destination in a Belted ACB Solution
summary: Process a full destination in the belted ACB flow by dropping the full destination bin onto the takeaway conveyor, allowing packages to move as a slug down the chute into the bag station, then labeling, zipping, and placing the completed bag on the takeaway belt.
when_to_use: Use when a destination is full in the belted ACB solution and the documented flow is to move the full destination bin onto the takeaway conveyor so packages can be conveyed as a slug into the bag station for bag handling.
not_for: Do not use for AGV/tipper OptiSweep bagging flows.

Do not use for traditional small sort bagging flows where the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute at the sorter location.
Do not use for fault recovery, jam recovery, or conveyor/bin-drop failures because the source does not provide those recovery steps.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Carry out the documented belted ACB bag flow at the bag station, including label application, zipping the bag, and placing the bag on the takeaway belt.

safety_notes: The source provides a high-level operating sequence only and does not provide detailed safety controls or recovery instructions.
If the conveyor-to-bag-station flow cannot be completed, stop and escalate because no fault handling is provided in the source.

access_required: Operator access to the belted ACB bag station flow
system_preconditions: A destination is full in the belted ACB solution.
The takeaway conveyor, chute, and bag station are available for the documented flow.

visual_references.description: Training slide comparing traditional small sort bagging, belted ACB, and Fortna OptiSweep-style flow; includes the belted ACB full-destination-to-bag-station description.

steps.title: Identify the full destination in the belted ACB solution
steps.purpose: Confirm that the documented belted ACB full-destination bag flow applies.
steps.instruction: Identify that the destination is full in the belted ACB solution before starting the bag flow.
steps.expected_result: A full destination requiring the belted ACB bag flow has been identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The destination is recognized as full and matches the belted ACB flow described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: It is not clear that the destination is full, or the situation does not match the belted ACB flow described in the source.

actions.label: Confirm full destination condition
actions.exact_action: Identify that the destination is full in the belted ACB solution.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The belted ACB portion of the comparison slide showing that when a destination is full, the bin is dropped onto a takeaway conveyor and conveyed to a bag station.

stop_or_escalate_if: The condition does not match a full destination in the belted ACB solution.
The operator cannot confirm the documented belted ACB flow from the available source evidence.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In a Belted ACB solution when a destination is full the bin is dropped on a takeaway conveyor that conveys the packages as a slug down a chute into a bag station.

steps.title: Drop the full destination bin onto the takeaway conveyor
steps.purpose: Start the documented belted ACB transfer from the full destination to the bag station flow.
steps.instruction: Drop the full destination bin onto the takeaway conveyor as described by the source.
steps.expected_result: The full destination bin is transferred onto the takeaway conveyor.
steps.healthy_condition: The bin is dropped onto the takeaway conveyor and the transfer begins as described.
steps.failure_condition: The bin does not drop onto the takeaway conveyor or the transfer cannot be completed.

actions.label: Drop full bin to conveyor
actions.exact_action: Drop the full destination bin onto the takeaway conveyor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The belted ACB flow description indicating the full destination bin is dropped onto a takeaway conveyor.

stop_or_escalate_if: The bin does not drop onto the takeaway conveyor.
The documented flow cannot continue and no recovery steps are provided by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In a Belted ACB solution when a destination is full the bin is dropped on a takeaway conveyor...

steps.title: Allow packages to convey as a slug into the bag station
steps.purpose: Move the packages from the full destination through the documented conveyor and chute path to the bag station.
steps.instruction: Allow the takeaway conveyor to convey the packages as a slug down the chute into the bag station.
steps.expected_result: Packages move as a slug down the chute and arrive at the bag station.
steps.healthy_condition: Packages are conveyed from the takeaway conveyor down the chute into the bag station as described.
steps.failure_condition: Packages do not convey correctly, do not move as a slug, or do not reach the bag station.

actions.label: Observe conveyor and chute flow
actions.exact_action: Allow the takeaway conveyor to convey the packages as a slug down the chute into the bag station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The belted ACB description showing packages conveyed as a slug down a chute into a bag station.

stop_or_escalate_if: Packages do not convey correctly to the bag station.
The conveyor-to-bag-station flow cannot be completed and the source provides no fault handling.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: ...conveys the packages as a slug down a chute into a bag station.

steps.title: Apply the label to the bag at the bag station
steps.purpose: Label the bag as part of the documented bag station handling flow.
steps.instruction: At the bag station, apply the label to the bag.
steps.expected_result: The bag has the label applied.
steps.healthy_condition: The bag is labeled at the bag station.
steps.failure_condition: The bag is not labeled or the label cannot be applied.

actions.label: Apply bag label
actions.exact_action: Apply the label to the bag.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: OCR text on the comparison slide describing bag handling actions including printing and applying a label.

stop_or_escalate_if: The bag cannot be labeled and the source provides no alternate handling.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR states that ... when a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt...

steps.title: Zip the bag
steps.purpose: Close the bag as part of the documented bag station flow.
steps.healthy_condition: The bag is zipped and ready for placement on the takeaway belt.
steps.failure_condition: The bag is not zipped or cannot be closed as described.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: OCR text on the slide listing the bagging action to zip the bag.

stop_or_escalate_if: The bag cannot be zipped and the source provides no recovery guidance.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR states that ... the operator ... zips the bag...

steps.title: Place the bag on the takeaway belt
steps.purpose: Complete the documented bag station handoff.
steps.instruction: Place the bag on the takeaway belt.
steps.expected_result: The completed bag is placed on the takeaway belt.
steps.healthy_condition: The labeled and zipped bag is placed on the takeaway belt.
steps.failure_condition: The bag is not placed on the takeaway belt or the handoff cannot be completed.

actions.label: Place bag on takeaway belt
actions.exact_action: Place the bag on the takeaway belt.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: OCR text on the slide listing placement of the bag on a takeaway belt.

stop_or_escalate_if: The bag cannot be placed on the takeaway belt.
The documented flow cannot be completed and the source provides no fault handling.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR states that ... the operator ... places it on a takeaway belt...

success_criteria: The full destination bin is dropped onto the takeaway conveyor.
Packages are conveyed as a slug down the chute into the bag station.
The bag is placed on the takeaway belt.
healthy_conditions: The belted ACB full-destination flow matches the source description.
Packages move from the full destination to the bag station through the takeaway conveyor and chute.
The completed bag is labeled, zipped, and handed off on the takeaway belt.
failure_conditions: The bin does not drop onto the takeaway conveyor.
Packages do not convey correctly to the bag station.
The bag cannot be labeled, zipped, or placed on the takeaway belt.
Any part of the conveyor-to-bag-station flow cannot be completed and the source provides no recovery steps.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the bin does not drop or packages do not convey correctly because the source does not provide recovery steps.
Escalate if the documented conveyor-to-bag-station flow cannot be completed because no fault handling is provided in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Comparison slide showing the belted ACB full-destination flow and bagging-related OCR text.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: In a Belted ACB solution when a destination is full the bin is dropped on a takeaway conveyor that conveys the packages as a slug down a chute into a bag station.

missing_details: The source does not provide detailed operator controls for how the bin is dropped onto the takeaway conveyor.
The source does not provide explicit fault handling, jam recovery, or exception steps for conveyor or bag station issues.
The source does not provide timing estimates.
The source does not explicitly define supporting roles or escalation contacts.
The source packet contains no commands, HMI navigation steps, or control panel instructions for this procedure.
